verdict.answer_first: A leads with the plan in sentence 1: "default I'd run with" and immediately says "TTL-based cache-aside with Redis"; B starts with "decision path" and delays the actual default until Step 3.
verdict.decisiveness: A commits to one default: "TTL-based cache-aside with Redis" and says when to upgrade; B surveys in-process, Redis, Memcached, TTL, write-through, replicas, pooling, so the prescription is diluted.
verdict.fabrication: none; both use conditional rules and marked heuristics, though B's "80% of read load is 10–20 distinct queries" and "70–80%" thresholds are plausible but unsourced.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to execute: "Set TTLs of 30–60s... add TTL jitter + a per-key lock... ship one query behind a flag first" plus "top 5 queries"; B is thorough but too many branches for a first plan.
